name: ddtrace (Datadog APM Python SDK)
pricing_notes: Open source (BSD-3-Clause); Datadog Inc. — Datadog subscription required for full observability

auth.notes: DD_API_KEY env var; DD_AGENT_HOST for local agent (default) or agentless mode

performance.notes: In-process tracing with ~1ms overhead per trace; async-safe

agent_readiness.notes: Datadog APM distributed tracing for Python. Automatic: ddtrace-run python app.py. Manual: from ddtrace import tracer. with tracer.trace('web.request', resource='/user', service='web') as span: span.set_tag('user.id', user_id); result = process_request(). Decorator: @tracer.wrap(service='my-svc'). dd_trace.patch_all() → auto-instruments Django, Flask, SQLAlchemy, requests, etc. Custom metrics: from datadog import statsd; statsd.increment('page.views'). Logs correlation: inject_trace_info() → adds trace_id to log record. Profiler: from ddtrace.profiling import Profiler; p = Profiler(); p.start().

gotchas: Datadog SaaS subscription required for full APM — ddtrace itself is open source
patch_all() must be called before imports to auto-instrument
Datadog Agent must be running locally or set DD_AGENT_HOST
Distributed tracing requires all services to use compatible tracers
Sampling configuration needed for high-throughput services to avoid costs
best_when: Datadog APM integration for distributed tracing across Python microservices
Auto-instrumentation of Django, Flask, FastAPI, SQLAlchemy
Correlating logs, metrics, and traces in Datadog
CPU/memory profiling with Datadog's continuous profiler
avoid_when: Budget-constrained projects where Datadog costs are prohibitive
summary: ddtrace is Datadog's Python APM SDK. ddtrace-run app.py for auto-instrumentation. Manual: tracer.trace('op', service='svc'). patch_all() instruments Django/Flask/SQLAlchemy/requests. Custom metrics via statsd. Log correlation. Continuous profiler. BSD-3 by Datadog. Best for Datadog APM with auto-instrumentation. Datadog subscription required for full APM. patch_all() before imports. Sampling needed for high traffic.
